tweets.text: A very confusing column—and a prime example of American exceptionalism—from NYT's @NickKristof which assumes that an action by China (military exercise) "elevate the peril" while an action by the US ($11 billion arms sale to Taiwan) that preceded China's action is "deterrence." https://t.co/xR13eL7HvQ
tweets.translation: 《纽约时报》@NickKristof 的一篇非常令人困惑的专栏文章——也是美国例外主义的典型例子——它假设中国的行动（军事演习）“升级了危险”，而先于中国行动的美国行动（向台湾出售110亿美元武器）则是“威慑”。

tweets.text: @NickKristof However, the $11 billion arms sale to Taiwan was announced by Trump on Dec. 18, as the NYT itself reported (https://t.co/whQlT4gP1E), while China held the military drills on Dec. 29 (https://t.co/HLAyNOTVIP).
tweets.translation: @NickKristof 然而，正如《纽约时报》自己报道的那样（https://t.co/whQlT4gP1E），特朗普是在12月18日宣布向台湾出售110亿美元武器的，而中国在12月29日举行了军事演习（https://t.co/HLAyNOTVIP）。

tweets.text: @NickKristof A casual NYT reader might think the US' $11 billion arms sale to Taiwan came after China's military exercises. This would be understandable, given that Kristof wrote of China's exercises before the US sale. He also didn't specify the dates ("late last month" and "in December").
tweets.translation: @NickKristof 随意的《纽约时报》读者可能会认为美国向台湾出售110亿美元武器是在中国军事演习之后。这是可以理解的，因为Kristof在写美国军售之前先写了中国的演习。他也没有具体说明日期（“上个月下旬”和“12月”）。

tweets.text: How an $11 billion arms sale to Taiwan, and the positioning of 500 troops in Taiwan in May 2025 (https://t.co/kwoBs2hqP1) exemplifies a policy of "ambiguity" beyond just being a "challenge to Beijing" is not considered by Campbell.
tweets.translation: 坎贝尔没有考虑到，向台湾出售110亿美元武器以及2025年5月在台湾部署500名军队（https://t.co/kwoBs2hqP1），除了是“对北京的挑战”之外，如何体现了一种“模糊”政策。
